title: caucalis praetermissa
intro: Caucalis praetermissa, also known as Lesser Wild Carrot or Wild Carrot, is a plant species within the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Caucalis praetermissa include reducing symptoms of menopause, alleviating digestive issues, and providing antioxidant properties to protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Caucalis praetermissa typically involve drying the roots or leaves to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
